<!-- PROSE — edit freely in Obsidian -->

A novella set during a brutal snowstorm on the Russian steppe. A wealthy merchant and his peasant servant lose their way and face death in the cold. As the night deepens, the merchant undergoes a spiritual transformation — sacrificing his own warmth to save his servant's life. One of Tolstoy's most concentrated moral parables, written in the years after his religious conversion.
